steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Text Structure and Purpose. The decisive clue is the sentence "Both centers contrast with museums that aren’t Indigenous-led" — the passage first presents two tribal centers, then shifts to compare them with non-Indigenous institutions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It outlines an early strategy for exhibit design used by one tribal cultural center, then explains a newer strategy used by a different tribal cultural center.: imposes a chronological early-vs.-newer distinction the text never makes; both tribal centers are presented as using the same approach, not different eras. ✗
  • Choice B — It discusses two cultural centers operated by tribes, then compares them with non-Indigenous institutions that present Indigenous exhibits.: accurately captures both halves of the text — the Pojoaque and Tohono O’odham centers first, then the contrast with non-Indigenous museums. ✓
  • Choice C — It describes how tribal cultural centers designed exhibits of a particular set of artifacts, then analyzes how non-Indigenous institutions designed exhibits of the same artifacts.: invents a "particular set of artifacts" never mentioned; the text discusses design philosophy and intended audiences, not a specific artifact collection. ✗
  • Choice D — It examines how tribal citizens respond to exhibits at tribal cultural centers, then speculates how non-Indigenous audiences would respond to the same exhibits.: shifts focus to audience response and speculation — the text says nothing about how visitors actually respond to exhibits. ✗
That is the correct answer.
